subject: [AAF SMS] Use certInitializer for certificates
commit_message:
[AAF SMS] Use certInitializer for certificates

AAF SMS was hardcoding its certificates in the container.
This patch makes use of certInitializer in order to retrieve "fresh"
certificates.
In order to use certInitiliazer in a sub component, we had to move
charts to component and add the right requirements.

Issue-ID: AAF-1159
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: I6ec55eddffd54dd56b03cea1a6f0b437f8bfa299

I'm trying to use usual format for key and certificate so we'll see if that's OK

Patch Set 5:

@Krzysztof, @Christophe, the patch seems to be fine (I took a look on gating3).
Can you look at it and gives me your point of view?

I'm not really happy by adding the Intermediate CA this way but as long as we don't update aaf "authz" by asking pkcs12 also for AAF-SMS (and I think it requires a new version of AAF) I believe we'll have to do that.

good news AAF-SMS, OOF error is fixed!

However we got new HC errors (see https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/108825-5/healthcheck/full/full/report.html#totals?all)
- we got a 500 on DCAE, which is an error that occurs time to time
no log from https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/108825-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-dcae-healthcheck-7965c86b54-zl75c.html
- connection error on dmaap bus controller HC, service is declared but there were some init errors
https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/108825-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/service-dmaap-bc.html
- timeout on 1 of the VFC HC (ems driver)
I cans see 20299 [alarmManager] DEBUG org.onap.vfc.nfvo.emsdriver.collector.alarm.AlarmManager  - The configuration properties from /service/emsdriver/conf/config.properties is not load
java.net.UnknownHostException: dcae-ves-collector
	at java.net.AbstractPlainSocketImpl.connect(AbstractPlainSocketImpl.java:184)
in the pod logs https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/108825-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-vfc-ems-driver-84995cd654-5d86m-vfc-ems-driver-logs.html so I assume it could be an induced effect of DCAE error

the End2End freeradius is PASS but seen as fail in CI due to an error when cleaning the resources
The End2End clearwater was Ok up to Openstack (Onboarding/distri/instantiation from ONAP perspective)
the End2End error is something we observe (404 from AAI when requesting PNF info)
 
So from a AAF-SMS perspective I think it is OK..I would prefer a full green but I cannot say if some of the errors could be a side effect
